response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable and broad individual contributor with over 15 years of engineering experience across various stacks, but he is fundamentally mismatched for a CTO role at this seniority and specialization level. He has no documented experience managing engineering teams of any size, no demonstrated track record in agentic AI systems at production scale, and only superficial fintech exposure. His recent work at re:cinq touches agentic tooling (MCP frameworks), but this is measured in months and described at a contributor level, not architectural leadership. The role requires someone who has already operated multi-agent AI systems in regulated financial environments and scaled engineering organizations — there is no evidence of either.
strengths: Genuine distributed-systems background with high-throughput microservices experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of requests/sec, low-latency DLang services)
Recent hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing awareness of the current AI-agent landscape
Broad polyglot technical foundation (TypeScript, DLang, C++, Node.js, embedded, mobile) indicating strong systems-level intuition
concerns: Zero evidence of engineering leadership: no team management, no org scaling, no development of technical leadership layers — always an individual contributor or solo open-source founder

Agentic AI experience is extremely thin and entirely recent (April 2025 onward), with no production multi-agent systems at scale, regulated environments, or financial-consequence deployments on record
Fintech exposure is minimal and peripheral — RIDE Capital involvement was as a senior developer applying TDD/CI practices, not architecting credit systems, payment flows, or risk infrastructure
